---

title: Research Data Management
metadata:
teaching: 40
exercises: 40
module: EDS-M4 – Research Data Management
course: Essential Digital Skills (EDS)
audience: Early Career Researchers (PhD, Postdoc)
-------------------------------------------------

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
![](fig/research-data-management-qr.jpg){fig-align="center" width=400px}
:::

::: callout-outcomes

## 💡 Learning Outcomes

* Define roles and responsibilities for managing research data at the University of Nottingham (UoN).
* Produce a concise Data Management Plan (DMP) covering storage, documentation, sharing, retention, and preservation.
* Apply robust naming conventions, folder structures, metadata, and version control for collaborative, auditable workflows.
* Select appropriate institutional storage and archiving solutions (e.g., OneDrive, SharePoint/Teams, shared drives, TRE secure storage).
* Assess and improve dataset reusability in line with **FAIR** principles (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable).
  :::

::: callout-questions

## ❓ Questions

1. Who is accountable for **long-term preservation** decisions?
2. What are the **essential sections** of a Data Management Plan (DMP)?
3. How would you design a **collaborative folder structure** and file-naming scheme?
4. Which actions most improve the **FAIRness** of a dataset?
   :::

## Structure & Agenda

1. RDM at UoN (~15 min)
2. Data Management Plans (~15 min)
3. Data Structuring, Metadata, and Archiving (~15 min)
4. Reusable Data in Practice (~15 min)

---

# RDM at UoN

Research Data Management (RDM) ensures that data are **organised, secure, documented, and preserved** to support research integrity, reproducibility, and reuse. Effective RDM is a shared responsibility across project roles and services.

## Roles and Responsibilities

| **Role**                        | **Key Responsibilities**                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Principal Investigator (PI)** | Accountable for project-level RDM, retention, and preservation. Ensures compliance with funder and institutional policies. |
| **Researchers**                 | Day-to-day organisation, secure handling, documentation, and quality control. Implement the DMP.                           |
| **Project Team**                | Shared responsibility for access, version control, and documentation.                                                      |
| **Library (RDM Support)**       | Advises on metadata, citation, archiving, and FAIR compliance.                                                             |
| **IT Services**                 | Provides secure storage, backup, and collaboration platforms.                                                              |
| **Information Security**        | Defines classification tiers and enforces access controls.                                                                 |

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    PI[Principal Investigator] -->|Oversees DMP & Preservation| T[Project Team]
    T -->|Implements Standards| R[Researchers]
    R -->|Coordinates Support| L[Library]
    L -->|Advises on Metadata| IT[IT Services]
    IT -->|Ensures Security| IS[Information Security]

    style PI fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#1565C0,stroke-width:1px
    style T fill:#E8F5E9,stroke:#2E7D32,stroke-width:1px
    style R fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#EF6C00,stroke-width:1px
    style L fill:#F3E5F5,stroke:#6A1B9A,stroke-width:1px
    style IT fill:#E0F7FA,stroke:#00838F,stroke-width:1px
    style IS fill:#F0F4C3,stroke:#9E9D24,stroke-width:1px
```

> 🧭 *Good RDM depends on collaboration and accountability across roles.*

## Code of Practice on RDM — Expectations

* Plan data **before** collection using a DMP; review regularly.
* Store in **approved platforms** and apply **data classification** and access controls.
* Maintain **metadata and documentation** sufficient for audit and reuse.
* Agree **retention and preservation** periods and identify archives.
* Enable **responsible sharing**; record reasons for restrictions.

## Institutional Storage Options

| **Platform**                         | **Purpose**                 | **Notes**                                 |
| ------------------------------------ | --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| **OneDrive**                         | Individual work area        | Automatic versioning and sync             |
| **SharePoint / Teams**               | Collaborative workspace     | Fine-grained permissions, version control |
| **CPS Research Storage** | Group-managed access        | Backed-up, scalable                       |
| **TRE Secure Storage**               | Sensitive/confidential data | Encryption, limited access, audit logs    |

> 🔐 *Choose storage based on sensitivity, access needs, and classification level.*

---

::: callout-task

#### Task 1: RDM Roles Quiz (10 min)

::: panel-tabset

##### Question

Match responsibilities to roles (PI, Researcher, Team, Library, IT, InfoSec).

##### Exercise

| Responsibility                             | PI | Researcher | Team | Library | IT | InfoSec |
| ------------------------------------------ | -- | ---------- | ---- | ------- | -- | ------- |
| Select retention/preservation strategy     |    |            |      |         |    |         |
| Maintain documentation                     |    |            |      |         |    |         |
| Provision storage/backups                  |    |            |      |         |    |         |
| Advise on metadata and archiving           |    |            |      |         |    |         |
| Enforce classification and access controls |    |            |      |         |    |         |
| Approve and review DMP                     |    |            |      |         |    |         |

##### Follow-up

* Which responsibilities are **shared**?
* What issues would you **escalate** and to whom?
  :::
  :::

---

# Data Management Plans

A **Data Management Plan (DMP)** is a living document that outlines what data will be produced, how they will be handled, and what will happen to them after project completion.

## Why DMPs Matter

| **Purpose**             | **Outcome**                                 |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Clarify expectations    | Reduces confusion and duplication           |
| Ensure compliance       | Meets funder and institutional requirements |
| Improve reproducibility | Ensures transparency and documentation      |
| Manage risk             | Prevents loss, breaches, and misuse         |

> 🗂️ *A DMP is not a formality—it is the blueprint for responsible research data management.*

## Funder Expectations & Institutional Template

Most funders (UKRI, Wellcome, Horizon Europe) require a DMP at proposal or project start. UoN’s DMP template includes:

1. Data description and anticipated volume.
2. Formats and standards.
3. Documentation and metadata approach.
4. Storage, backup, and security.
5. Legal, ethical, and IP considerations.
6. Sharing, reuse, and licensing.
7. Retention and preservation strategy.
8. Roles, responsibilities, and costs.

---

::: callout-task

#### Task 2: DMP Scenario Challenge (10 min)

::: panel-tabset

##### Question

Complete a mini-DMP for the following scenario.

##### Scenario

A mixed-methods project collects:

* Semi-structured **interviews** (audio and transcripts) with NHS staff.
* A **survey** (pseudonymised) on workflows.
* **Derived datasets** and **analysis scripts** (R/Python).
  The team spans multiple institutions and handles sensitive data.

##### Mini-DMP Template

* **Data & Formats:**
* **Documentation & Metadata:**
* **Storage & Access Controls:**
* **Legal/Ethical Considerations:**
* **Sharing & Licensing:**
* **Retention & Preservation:**
* **Roles & Responsibilities:**

##### Follow-up

* Identify **risks** and **mitigations** (pseudonymisation, encryption, DP agreements).
* Specify **archival location** and **licensing** for outputs.
  :::
  :::

---

# Data Structuring, Metadata, and Archiving

Good RDM depends on structured, consistent organisation. Logical file structures and descriptive metadata improve collaboration and prevent loss.

## Naming Conventions

Recommended pattern:

```
YYYYMMDD_project-feature_description_vNN.ext
```

**Examples**

```
20250112_survey_raw_responses_v01.csv
20250115_audio_int01_redacted_v02.wav
20250120_analysis_regression_results_v03.parquet
```

> 🗃️ *Names should sort chronologically, describe content clearly, and indicate versioning.*

## Folder Structure Example

```
project-name/
  00-admin/
  01-data/
    raw/
    interim/
    processed/
  02-analysis/
    scripts/
    notebooks/
    results/
  03-docs/
    README.md
    CODEBOOK.md
    CHANGES.md
  04-outputs/
  99-archive/
```

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    A[Raw Data] --> B[Interim]
    B --> C[Processed]
    C --> D[Outputs]
    D --> E[Archive]

    style A fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#1565C0,stroke-width:1px
    style B fill:#E8F5E9,stroke:#2E7D32,stroke-width:1px
    style C fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#EF6C00,stroke-width:1px
    style D fill:#F3E5F5,stroke:#6A1B9A,stroke-width:1px
    style E fill:#F0F4C3,stroke:#9E9D24,stroke-width:1px
```

## Metadata and Documentation

| **Document**        | **Purpose**                                  |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| **README.md**       | Overview of project and data organisation    |
| **CODEBOOK.md**     | Defines variables, units, and coding schemes |
| **CHANGES.md**      | Records version history and transformations  |
| **DATA DICTIONARY** | Lists fields, formats, provenance            |

> 🧩 *Metadata connects data to context and enables reuse.*

## Storage, Versioning, and Archiving

* Use **approved institutional storage** (OneDrive, Teams, CPS, TRE).
* Maintain clear separation between **raw** and **processed** data.
* Apply **version control** for scripts and datasets.
* Archive **final datasets** with documentation and assign a DOI.

---

::: callout-task

#### Task 3: Folder Design (10 min)

::: panel-tabset

##### Question

Design a folder tree and naming convention for a collaborative project.

##### Exercise

Create a structure based on:

```
project-name/
  00-admin/
  01-data/ raw/ interim/ processed/
  02-analysis/ scripts/ notebooks/ results/
  03-docs/ README.md CODEBOOK.md CHANGES.md
  04-outputs/
  99-archive/
```

Add **three example filenames** using the pattern `YYYYMMDD_project-feature_description_vNN.ext`.

##### Follow-up

* Assign storage locations for each folder.
* Define access permissions.
* Identify which folders will be archived.
  :::
  :::

---

# Reusable Data in Practice

Reusability underpins the **FAIR** principles—Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable. Data that follow these principles enhance transparency and long-term value.

## FAIR Principles Overview

| **Principle**     | **Key Features**                                      | **Example Practice**          |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| **Findable**      | Persistent identifiers (DOI, ORCID), indexed metadata | Dataset DOIs, ORCID iDs       |
| **Accessible**    | Stable links, access conditions                       | Repository landing pages      |
| **Interoperable** | Open formats, standard vocabularies                   | CSV + JSON + ontology mapping |
| **Reusable**      | Clear licences, provenance, documentation             | CC BY licence + CODEBOOK.md   |

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Findable] --> B[Accessible] --> C[Interoperable] --> D[Reusable] --> A

    style A fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#1565C0,stroke-width:1px
    style B fill:#E8F5E9,stroke:#2E7D32,stroke-width:1px
    style C fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#EF6C00,stroke-width:1px
    style D fill:#F3E5F5,stroke:#6A1B9A,stroke-width:1px
```

## Persistent Identifiers and Licensing

* Use **DOIs** for datasets and **ORCID iDs** for contributors.
* Select licences suited to context (e.g., **CC BY**, **CC BY-NC**, **ODC-BY**).
* Record and communicate third-party restrictions clearly.

## Repositories and Archiving

* Prefer **institutional or domain repositories**.
* Provide **complete metadata** even where data cannot be open.
* Archive sensitive data under **controlled access**; publish descriptive metadata.
* Maintain **persistent links** between datasets, code, and publications.

> 🔁 *FAIR data enhance reproducibility and reduce duplication across research domains.*

---

::: callout-task

#### Task 4: FAIRness Audit (10 min)

::: panel-tabset

##### Question

Assess a dataset for FAIRness and propose improvements.

##### Checklist

* [ ] PID (DOI) or stable citation
* [ ] Machine-readable metadata
* [ ] Open, documented formats
* [ ] Declared licence
* [ ] Provenance and processing steps
* [ ] Access method and responsible contact
* [ ] Links to related outputs (e.g., code, publications)

##### Follow-up

* Suggest **three concrete actions** to improve FAIRness.
  :::
  :::

---

# Further Information

* Research Data Management (Standalone) — complementary training
* [Code of Practice on Research Data Management](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/library/research/research-data-management/index.aspx)
* [Code of Research Conduct and Research Ethics](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/ethics-and-integrity/)
* Annual **Information Security Awareness** training (mandatory).

---

::: callout-keypoints

## 📚 Keypoints

* Plan early using a DMP and review at milestones.
* Use approved storage, structured folders, and meaningful names.
* Maintain documentation and metadata to support reuse.
* Decide retention and preservation routes before project end.
* Aim for FAIR data; share as openly as possible with justified restrictions.
  :::

::: callout-hints

## 🔦 Hints

* Keep DMPs short, specific, and actionable.
* Treat README and CODEBOOK as living documents.
* Separate raw and processed data.
* Prefer open formats and controlled vocabularies.
* Publish metadata even when data cannot be shared.
  :::
